name: OpenClaw Research Evaluation Workflow
goal: Ensure OpenClaw can install, inspect, validate, and execute research evaluation cases in a deterministic order.
non_negotiables: Do not replace a required real artifact with a Markdown summary unless the case explicitly defines Markdown as the main target artifact.
If a case expects DOCX, PPTX, XLSX, MP4, CSV, or JSON, the run is incomplete until that exact artifact exists.
If a required skill is not installed, install it first; do not simulate its behavior with generic text output.
If a required skill needs configuration, finish configuration before claiming the case is executable.
A report about an artifact is not the artifact itself.
preflight: Confirm the project is installed with `py -m pip install -e .`.
Initialize the workspace with `py -m openclaw_eval.cli init`.
Use `py -m openclaw_eval.cli list-cases` to inspect available cases.
Use `py -m openclaw_eval.cli show-case <CASE_ID>` to read the selected case.

execution_steps.name: Initialize workspace
execution_steps.action: Create workspace folders and skill templates.

execution_steps.name: Select case
execution_steps.action: List cases and choose a case ID.
execution_steps.success_check: A valid case ID is selected.

execution_steps.name: Inspect case requirements
execution_steps.action: Open the structured case definition and read required skills, outputs, and criteria.
execution_steps.success_check: The required skill list, output artifacts, and output rules are known.

execution_steps.name: Verify skill installation and configuration
execution_steps.action: For every required skill, ensure SKILL.md and manifest.json both exist, then inspect configuration requirements.
execution_steps.command: Manual file check or create folders from workspace/docs/skill_template, then run `py -m openclaw_eval.cli show-skill <slug>`
execution_steps.success_check: All required skills are installed and all required configuration items are known.

execution_steps.name: Run readiness evaluation
execution_steps.action: Generate a dry-run report before attempting actual execution.
execution_steps.success_check: A JSON report and Markdown report are generated under workspace/outputs/reports.

execution_steps.name: Read report and decide next action
execution_steps.action: If any skill is missing, incomplete, or unconfigured, fix it first. For non-Markdown targets, require the actual artifact file to be produced before claiming completion.
execution_steps.success_check: There is a clear decision: fix missing skills/configuration or move to real execution.

decision_rules: If the case ID is missing, stop and choose a valid case.
If any required skill is not available, do not claim the case is executable.
If a skill folder exists but SKILL.md or manifest.json is missing, classify it as incomplete installation.
If a skill has required configuration items, do not classify it as ready until those items are configured.
Always produce a dry-run report before any real execution.
Only treat a case as ready when all required skills pass verification.
Do not accept Markdown commentary as a substitute for real outputs like DOCX, PPTX, XLSX, MP4, CSV, or JSON.
